auth.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please log in again.

admin.title: Administration
admin.envTrackerDesc: Manage deployment environments and reservation policy. Repositories come automatically from Jira tickets.
admin.envJiraFilterHint: Choose which projects, issue types and statuses appear when reserving an environment. Leave empty to skip that filter.
admin.envReservationPickerEmpty: No tickets match the configured filters. Check Admin → Environments → Jira filter.
admin.envStatusesHint: Drag to reorder. The category drives behavior (reserved/in use/final/cancelled/violation).
admin.envHistoryNoteHint: This text is shown below the history table. You can use basic HTML (bold, lists, links).
admin.envPriorityHint: Lower = appears first in the sidebar
admin.envAddJiraKey: Add at least one Jira key.
admin.envConfirmCancel: Are you sure you want to cancel this reservation?
admin.envNoGanttData: No reservations with dates to show on the Gantt.
admin.envHistoryNoRecords: No records in the last 2 months.
admin.systemRole: The admin role has full access and cannot be modified
admin.tokenHint: Generate at id.atlassian.com → Security → API tokens
admin.csvDropzone: Drop CSV here or click to browse
admin.asFixedHint: Seat will be locked for this person permanently

jiraTracker.title: Jira Tracker
jiraTracker.jiraTokenDesc: Set your token so your time logs appear under your name in Jira.
jiraTracker.exportHint: Only hours within the selected range
jiraTracker.noWorklogsSub: Try another day or user filter
jiraTracker.timeWarn: That's a lot! Are you sure you want to log {{h}}h?

hotdesk.title: HotDesk
hotdesk.noBlueprint: No floor plan configured for this floor
hotdesk.noReserve: This seat has a fixed assignment.
hotdesk.alreadyReserved: You already have a reservation for this date.
hotdesk.confirmBookingDesc: Confirm you are at your desk today
hotdesk.delegateSeatDesc: Let someone else use your fixed seat
hotdesk.scrollZoomHint: Scroll to zoom, drag to pan
hotdesk.checkInDesc: You have a pending reservation for today. Confirm your presence.
hotdesk.selectBuildingFloor: Select a building and floor above to see the map

retro.title: RetroBoard
retro.configSub: Configure the session before the team joins
retro.cardPrivate: Cards are private until the moderator closes the phase
retro.noTeam: You don't belong to any retro team.
retro.selectTeamPrompt: Select a team and enter a name to continue
retro.completeRetroHint: Complete a retrospective to see actionables here
retro.retroDesc: Run structured retrospectives: create cards, vote, define actionables.

deployPlanner.title: Deploy Planner
deployPlanner.subtaskDesc: Define which Jira issue types are considered subtasks and how to classify them.
deployPlanner.closedStatusesHint: Jira statuses that indicate the subtask is closed

chrono.title: Time Clock
chrono.label: Label
chrono.fichajePendiente: You have incomplete records pending completion
chrono.proximaVista: This section will be available soon
chrono.fechaFinAnterior: End date cannot be before start date
chrono.confirmarCancelar: Are you sure you want to cancel this request?
chrono.confirmarEliminar: Are you sure you want to delete this alarm?
chrono.dashboardDesc: Your automated time-tracking sync is active. System logs are stable and synchronized.
chrono.confirmarSalidaMsg: You have worked {{hours}}. Do you want to clock out?
chrono.deadlineWarning: You have 48 hours to complete incomplete records
chrono.deadlineDetail: After that period, they will be flagged as incidents and require HR approval.
chrono.alarmasSubtitulo: Set up alarms and reminders for your workday

profile.title: My profile
profile.subtitle: Your personal and account information
profile.name: Name
profile.comingSoon: Coming soon: hour stats, vacation balance and more

chronoAdmin.aprobacionesDesc: Review and manage pending approval requests
chronoAdmin.fichajesEquipoDesc: View time records for the entire team
chronoAdmin.razonRechazoDesc: Provide a reason for the rejection so the employee can correct it
chronoAdmin.confirmarEliminar: Are you sure you want to delete?
chronoAdmin.dashboardSubtitle: Team overview and clock records summary
chronoAdmin.jiraSubtitle: Jira hours vs clock hours comparison
chronoAdmin.reminderBulkMessage: Reminders will be sent to all employees with a deficit
chronoAdmin.reminderMessage: You have {{horas}} of incomplete clock-ins. Please review them.

vectorLogic.title: Vector Logic
vectorLogic.oneOpenWarning: Only one OPEN state allowed per workflow
vectorLogic.publishGuard: Workflow needs at least one OPEN and one DONE state to publish
vectorLogic.dropFieldHere: Drag a field from the library
vectorLogic.selectFieldToEdit: Select a field to edit it
vectorLogic.noTaskTypesAssigned: No task types with assigned workflow
vectorLogic.goToAssignmentManager: Go to Assignment Manager to link a workflow to a task type
vectorLogic.workflowHasNoStates: This workflow has no states configured yet
vectorLogic.aiSettingsDesc: Configure AI provider, API key and system prompt. The AI can create tasks and read emails via MCP.
vectorLogic.apiKeyWarning: ⚠️ The key is stored in the database. In production it should be proxied through a backend to avoid exposure in the browser.
vectorLogic.systemPromptDesc: Base instructions the AI receives on every conversation
vectorLogic.mcpEndpointDesc: URL of the MCP server exposing email-reading tools. Leave empty to disable.
vectorLogic.chatHint: Ask the AI about your tasks, request it to check emails via MCP, or have it create actionable items in your workspace.
vectorLogic.configureAiFirst: Configure the AI provider and API key in Settings first
vectorLogic.ruleDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe in natural language what the AI should do. E.g.: When an email from @client.com contains 'urgent' in the subject, create a Bug task with high priority.
vectorLogic.ruleDescriptionHint: The AI reads these rules on every conversation and applies them automatically when processing emails or interacting with you.
vectorLogic.aiRulesDesc: Define natural language rules the AI will follow when processing emails and creating tasks. These are passed as context on every conversation.
vectorLogic.noRulesHint: Create a rule so the AI knows how to process your emails
vectorLogic.loadModelsFirst: Paste your API key and click "Load models"
vectorLogic.mcpDesc: Vector Logic exposes itself as an MCP server. Connect your Claude client (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor) and talk to your workspace using your existing subscription — no separate API billing. Claude uses the tools below to read and modify Vector Logic.
vectorLogic.mcpTokenWarning: ⚠ This token expires with your session. If it expires, come back here and generate a new one.
vectorLogic.mcpClaudeCodeDesc: Run this command in your terminal:
vectorLogic.mcpStepDesktop4: Restart Claude Desktop. Vector Logic will appear as an active MCP server.
vectorLogic.toolListStates: List states for a given workflow
vectorLogic.toolListTaskTypes: List task types (with workflow and schema)
vectorLogic.toolListTasks: List tasks, optionally filtered by type, state or assignee
vectorLogic.toolCreateTask: Create a new task in the initial OPEN state
vectorLogic.toolUpdateTask: Update title, priority, or custom fields
vectorLogic.toolMoveTask: Move a task to a different state
vectorLogic.modeEmbeddedDesc: Talk to AI inside Vector Logic
vectorLogic.modeEmbeddedHint: The chat appears in the sidebar. Uses the provider you configure below (free Gemini, or your Claude/ChatGPT API key). The Chat tab is visible.
vectorLogic.modeExternalDesc: Use Claude Desktop or Claude Code via MCP
vectorLogic.modeExternalHint: The embedded chat is hidden. You connect your Claude client (Desktop or Code) to the Vector Logic MCP server following the steps in the MCP Access tab. Uses your existing Claude subscription.
vectorLogic.modeExternalSetup: To configure the Claude client, go to the MCP Access tab. There you'll find the endpoint, session token and config snippets for Claude Desktop and Claude Code.
vectorLogic.newTaskTypeHint: Will be created with default fields: Title, Description, Assignee, Start date and Due date. You can add, edit or remove fields afterwards.
vectorLogic.noTaskTypesYet: No task types yet. Create one to get started.
vectorLogic.emailRulesDesc: Define which emails the AI processes and how. Only emails matching an active rule will be analyzed.
vectorLogic.aiDetectionsDesc: Emails processed by the AI — review proposals before they become tasks.
vectorLogic.atLeastOneFilter: Add at least one filter criteria
vectorLogic.noEmailRulesHint: Create a rule to start processing emails from Gmail
vectorLogic.optionalActionsHint: Leave blank to let the AI decide. Set to force a specific task type or priority for every email matching this rule.
vectorLogic.noGmailConnectedHint: Connect your Gmail account in Settings to start receiving AI-detected tasks.
vectorLogic.noDetectionsHint: Once Gmail polling starts, matching emails will appear here.
vectorLogic.missingTaskType: Pick a task type before approving
vectorLogic.rejectDetectionConfirm: Reject this detection? It won't come back.
vectorLogic.gmailNotConfiguredServer: Server-side Google OAuth credentials are not configured. Contact the admin to set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET and GOOGLE_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI.
vectorLogic.gmailConnectHint: Connect your Gmail to let the AI read new emails and turn actionable ones into tasks.
vectorLogic.disconnectGmailConfirm: Disconnect Gmail? Existing tasks are kept; polling will stop.
vectorLogic.gmailError: Something went wrong during Google sign-in. Please try again.
vectorLogic.confidenceThresholdHint: Emails at or above this confidence auto-create a task. Below it, they wait in the review inbox. Drop to 0% for fully automatic.
vectorLogic.stateInUseError: This state is used in one or more workflows. Remove it from workflows first before deleting.
